ingredients.item: 1¾ ounces (50 g) dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked
ingredients.original: 1¾ ounces (50 g) dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked

ingredients.item: 1½ teaspoons vegetable or mushroom stock powder, divided
ingredients.original: 1½ teaspoons vegetable or mushroom stock powder, divided

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) sweet leaf bush (see Note) or spinach, stems removed and washed (optional)
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) sweet leaf bush (see Note) or spinach, stems removed and washed (optional)

ingredients.item: 10½ ounces (300 g) sponge gourd, ridge gourd, or zucchini, peeled and cut along the grain into half-moon slices
ingredients.original: 10½ ounces (300 g) sponge gourd, ridge gourd, or zucchini, peeled and cut along the grain into half-moon slices

ingredients.item: 1. Cut the rehydrated shiitake mushrooms into strips and set aside.
ingredients.original: 1. Cut the rehydrated shiitake mushrooms into strips and set aside.

ingredients.item: 2. In a small bowl, combine the leek, ½ teaspoon of the stock powder, soy sauce, and pepper. Mix well and set aside for 15 minutes.
ingredients.original: 2. In a small bowl, combine the leek, ½ teaspoon of the stock powder, soy sauce, and pepper. Mix well and set aside for 15 minutes.

ingredients.item: 3. Heat the oil in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. When hot, add the leek mixture and cook and stir until fragrant (about 30 seconds). Add mushrooms and cook and stir until tender (about 2 minutes).
ingredients.original: 3. Heat the oil in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. When hot, add the leek mixture and cook and stir until fragrant (about 30 seconds). Add mushrooms and cook and stir until tender (about 2 minutes).

ingredients.item: 4. Add the water to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Add sweet leaf bush (if using), sponge gourd, and the remaining 1 teaspoon stock powder. Let soup boil for 2 minutes longer, or until sponge gourd is soft and silky and sweet leaf bush is tender. Remove from heat.
ingredients.original: 4. Add the water to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Add sweet leaf bush (if using), sponge gourd, and the remaining 1 teaspoon stock powder. Let soup boil for 2 minutes longer, or until sponge gourd is soft and silky and sweet leaf bush is tender. Remove from heat.

ingredients.item: 5. Transfer to bowls and serve hot.
ingredients.original: 5. Transfer to bowls and serve hot.
